evidence_source: Fresh headless no-analysis run rs2quick60 over win64c/original/rs2client.exe for exact C-string reads.
Fresh headless no-analysis run rs2quick61 with targeted disassembly before GhidraFindRefsPy operand scans.

evidence: rs2quick61 GhidraDumpWindowsPy shows 0x140679577 loading this address into RDX before the 0x140679500 selector.
rs2quick61 GhidraDumpWindowsPy shows 0x1406797EF loading this address into RDX before the 0x140679720 sibling selector.
rs2quick61 GhidraFindRefsPy reports operand hits at 0x140679577 and 0x1406797EF after the windows are decoded.

0x140B74258.role: Record-builder one-case label root selected when record kind equals 1.
evidence: rs2quick61 GhidraDumpWindowsPy shows 0x140679582 loading this address into RAX before 0x1406795B0 CMOVZ.
rs2quick61 GhidraDumpWindowsPy shows 0x1406797E8 loading this address into RAX before 0x1406797FB CMOVZ.
rs2quick61 GhidraFindRefsPy reports operand hits at 0x140679582 and 0x1406797E8 after the windows are decoded.

0x140B5E03C.role: Hyphen fallback root used by both builders when their respective hyphen override byte is non-zero.
evidence: rs2quick61 GhidraFindRefsPy reports 0x14067960C/0x14067961B in 0x140679500 and 0x14067984C/0x14067985B in 0x140679720.

label_composition_model.statement: Both record builders create a temporary factory label by copying one of GlobalSettings, Settings, or '-' and then appending the caller-provided suffix such as totp or login.
examples: For 0x140679500, the known 0x19E90 callers append totp and login, so the factory label family includes GlobalSettings/Settings plus totp/login depending on record kind and hyphen override.
For 0x140679720, the fixed [record] = 1 path normally selects Settings before appending its caller-provided suffix, unless stack_arg8 forces '-' first.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-record-builder-factory-mo: Replaces the zero-case/one-case placeholder roots with concrete literal names: GlobalSettings and Settings.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-sibling-record-builder-ca: Confirms the fixed-kind sibling normally selects Settings while retaining the same '-' override behavior.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-entry-factory-callsite-ar: Improves the factory dedup-key model by naming the label root component that participates in the label text comparison.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-cache-bootstrap-flag-and-: Keeps the non-zero +0x28A bootstrap conclusion intact while making the produced settings/cache entry names easier to reason about.

tool_feedback_loop.tooling_note: GhidraFindRefsPy operand scans only see decoded instructions in quick -noanalysis projects. README.md now documents the workflow: run a targeted GhidraDumpWindowsPy window first, then run GhidraFindRefsPy for operand-level hits.
tool_feedback_loop.no_new_helper_needed: The existing GhidraReadCStringPy and GhidraFindRefsPy helpers covered this milestone once the no-analysis decoding order was correct.

bounded_inference.statement: The settings record-builder family is now a named Settings/GlobalSettings entry factory path, not an anonymous root-selector path. The selector bit and record kind choose Settings versus GlobalSettings, while a caller-controlled byte can collapse the root to '-' before the suffix append.

next_targets: Trace concrete code callers of 0x140679720 through the table reference at 0x140EEE27C to recover its suffix and provider object inputs.
Resolve the 0x140679720 stack_arg7 provider object and vtable slot invoked at 0x1406797DC.
Follow the Settings/GlobalSettings factory labels into the dedup tree comparison in 0x14067C1F0 to recover representative fully composed keys.
